title: NLPRank at OpenDNS
description: We're running a joint meetup with OpenLate, an OpenDNS night! (http://www.meetup.com/OpenLate/events/219866467/) Jeremiah O'Connnor, a researcher at OpenDNS, is presenting about "The Language of Evil: Hunting Malicious Domains with Natural Language Processing in Network Traffic." Domain squatting on legitimate brand names such as Google, Adobe, Java, and Paypal is a common tactic used by a wide array of adversaries, from mass phishing campaigns to spamming to C2 domains in APT attacks. In this talk, we present NLP-Rank, a generic detection model we have developed at OpenDNS Labs that utilizes NLP/Text processing techniques to identify malicious behavior in network traffic. Learn more about NLPRank on the OpenDNS research blog: https://labs.opendns.com/2015/03/05/nlp-apt-dns/
